function.name: calculate
function.description: Calculate the result of a mathematical expression.

expression.description: The mathematical expression to calculate, such as '2 + 2'. The expression can contain numbers, operators (+, -, *, /), parentheses, and spaces.

function.name: think
function.description: Use the tool to think about something. It will not obtain new information or change the database, but just append the thought to the log. Use it when complex reasoning or some cache memory is needed.

function.name: find_customer_by_email
function.description: Find a customer by their email address.

email.description: The customer's email address, such as 'john.smith@email.com'.

function.name: find_customer_by_phone
function.description: Find a customer by their phone number.

phone.description: The customer's phone number, such as '(555) 123-4567'.

function.name: get_customer_details
function.description: Get detailed information about a customer, including their account, services, and devices.

customer_id.description: The customer's unique identifier, such as 'first_last_1234'.

function.name: get_service_details
function.description: Get detailed information about a telecom service.

service_id.description: The service identifier, such as 'mobile_unlimited' or 'internet_fiber_1gb'.

function.name: get_device_details
function.description: Get detailed information about a device on the market

device_name.description: The name of the device to get details about.Options: iPhone 15 ProiPhone 14iPhone 13Samsung Galaxy S23iPhone 12iPhone 15Google Pixel 8Samsung Galaxy A54iPhone SE (3rd gen)WiFi 6 RouterStandard WiFi RouterEnterprise RouterBasic WiFi RouterSamsung 65" Smart TVHD Cable Box55" Smart TVConference Room TVConference Phone SystemHome Security System

function.name: get_billing_details
function.description: Get billing information for a customer, including current balance, payment history, and monthly charges.

customer_id.description: The customer's unique identifier, such as 'john_smith_1234'.

function.name: manage_service
function.description: Manage customer services: add, remove, suspend, activate, or list services.

customer_id.description: The customer's unique identifier, such as 'john_smith_1234'.

service_id.description: Service identifier (required for add/remove/suspend/activate), such as 'mobile_unlimited'.

devices_ids.description: The device ids. Should be a list of strings corresponding to the device ids in the customer's list of devices.

function.name: troubleshoot_device
function.description: Provide troubleshooting steps for device issues.

device_name.description: The device name, such as 'iPhone 15 Pro' or 'WiFi 6 Router'.

issue.description: Description of the issue with the device. Options: no_service, slow_speeds, battery_drain.

function.name: create_support_ticket
function.description: Create a new support ticket for a customer.

customer_id.description: The customer's unique identifier, such as 'john_smith_1234'.

priority.description: Priority level: 'low', 'medium', 'high', or 'urgent'. Defaults to 'medium'.                             Low is non-urgent, medium is standard, high is important, and urgent is critical (within 24 hours)

function.name: get_support_ticket_details
function.description: Get details of a support ticket.

ticket_id.description: The support ticket identifier, such as 'TICKET001'.

function.name: add_device
function.description: Add a device to a customer.

customer_id.description: The customer's unique identifier, such as 'john_smith_1234'.

device_name.description: The name of the device to add.Options: iPhone 15 ProiPhone 14iPhone 13Samsung Galaxy S23iPhone 12iPhone 15Google Pixel 8Samsung Galaxy A54iPhone SE (3rd gen)WiFi 6 RouterStandard WiFi RouterEnterprise RouterBasic WiFi RouterSamsung 65" Smart TVHD Cable Box55" Smart TVConference Room TVConference Phone SystemHome Security System

function.name: manage_billing
function.description: Manage customer billing: paperless, auto_pay, or billing_cycle.

customer_id.description: The customer's unique identifier, such as 'john_smith_1234'.

billing_cycle.description: The billing cycle. Should be a string. Options are: monthly, quarterly, annual.

function.name: get_services
function.description: Get detailed information about all telecom services.

function.name: get_senior_discount
function.description: Apply a fixed $5 senior discount to the provided service price.

original_price.description: Original price before applying the senior discount, e.g. '95.00'.

function.name: modify_support_ticket
function.description: Modify the status and priority of a support ticket.

ticket_id.description: The support ticket identifier, such as 'TICKET001'.

status.description: The status of the support ticket, such as 'open' or 'closed'.

priority.description: The priority of the support ticket, such as 'low', 'medium', 'high', or 'urgent'.

function.name: record_payment
function.description: Apply a payment to a customer account and update the remaining balance.

method.description: Payment method used. Options: credit_card, bank_transfer  .

date.description: Payment date in ISO format, e.g. '2025-09-18'.

reason_matrix: No — this is the same function as the target; self-edges are not allowed.
Yes — the computed result can be embedded into the 'thought' string to log what was calculated.
No — this is an entry-point requiring an email; the calculation result is not an email and entry points have no incoming edges.
No — this is an entry-point requiring a phone number; the calculation result is not a phone and entry points have no incoming edges.
No — requires a customer_id, which is not produced by a math calculation.
No — requires a service_id string; a numeric calculation result doesn’t provide this.
No — requires a device_name; the calculation output is not used here.
No — requires a customer_id; the calculation result is unrelated.
No — requires customer_id/action (and optionally service/devices); the calculation output isn’t used.
No — requires device_name and issue; a numeric calculation result doesn’t apply.
No — requires customer_id and category (and optional priority); the calculation result is not used.
No — requires a ticket_id; the calculation result is not a ticket identifier.
No — requires customer_id and device_name; the calculation output is irrelevant.
No — expects booleans and a billing_cycle; the numeric calculation result isn’t an input here.
No — this function takes no inputs and does not consume or depend on the calculation result.
Yes — the calculated value can be used as the original_price (converted to string) to apply the $5 discount.
No — requires ticket_id/status/priority; the calculation result isn’t used as any of these.
Yes — the calculated numeric result can directly be used as the 'amount' when recording a payment.
Think only appends an internal thought to a log and does not output a calculable expression for calculate(expression), so no direct I/O link.
Self-edge is not allowed; the target function cannot be its own neighbour.
This is an entry/lookup by email and requires an email; Think produces no email output and generally has no incoming edges.
This is an entry/lookup by phone and requires a phone; Think produces no phone output and generally has no incoming edges.

Requires service_id; Think does not output a service_id.
Requires device_name; Think does not output a device_name.

RecordPayment’s output (updated balance/receipt) isn’t used by a generic math calculator; no execution link.
Thought logging doesn’t depend on payment results and takes no related inputs.
Entry-point lookup by email cannot have incoming edges; payment output isn’t an input here.

Service metadata lookup is unrelated to recording a payment.

Managing add/remove/list services isn’t driven by the payment outcome and shares no inputs from it.
Device troubleshooting is unrelated to payment results.
If the payment fails or a discrepancy is detected, the payment result directly triggers creating a billing support ticket for the customer.
Payment outcome can prompt checking an existing billing ticket’s details (e.g., to verify resolution), so execution is directly influenced though no fields are passed.
Adding a device isn’t driven by payment results and shares no inputs from it.
Billing preferences (paperless/auto_pay/cycle) don’t take payment output as input; not directly triggered by recording a payment.
Listing service catalog isn’t related to recording payments.
Senior discount calculation on a price doesn’t use or depend on payment results.
